_comment: Fixed vibe vocabulary. Every bar.vibe_tags entry MUST be one of these strings. Every user's vibe preference MUST be a weight over this vocabulary. Version 2.1 trimmed three never-used tags (industry / no-cover / sidewalk-seating) flagged by the eval; v2.0 retuned for a 20-something NYC audience and split overloaded tags.

notes.opposing_pairs: Used by the scoring engine to detect incoherent vibe profiles — if a user weights both sides of an opposing pair highly, the system flags this in the explanation.
notes.split_rationale: In v1, 'dancing' lumped DJ sets, live bands, and actual dance floors into one signal — three very different experiences. Same for 'outdoor' (patio vs rooftop vs sidewalk) and 'themed' (tiki vs retro vs literary vs theatrical).
notes.deleted_in_v2: lgbtq-friendly (too vague; replaced by queer-centered which signals a primary draw, not just a welcoming space), dancing (split), outdoor (split), themed (split), chill (overlap with unpretentious), first-meet (subset of date), rowdy / loud / bright (redundant with noise_level ordinal or rarely used).
notes.deleted_in_v2_1: industry (never tagged on any bar; replaced by detecting via specials.industry_night), no-cover (rarely meaningful — most bars are walk-in already), sidewalk-seating (subsumed by patio).
notes.weighting_advice: For a 20-something audience, Activity-facet vibes (dance-floor, games, live-band, dj-set) carry more decision weight than Atmosphere vibes — people will happily go to a dive OR a cocktail bar for the right activity.
notes.no_drink_type_vibes_except_specific: Drink preferences (beer/wine/cocktails/whiskey/spirits/shots) live on Bar.drink_categories_served and UserPreference.preferred_drinks. Exception: craft-cocktails and natural-wine are genuine scene signals — a 'natural-wine bar' is a different vibe from a 'wine bar'.
